name: Chen Dan (Hangong)
page_title: Chen Dan (Hangong) 陳耽 (漢公) [Han] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes: Chen Dan was a high official under the Han. He served as Minister of Ceremonies, but was often promoted to various other ranks. Chen Dan tried to make a report after being promoted to Minister over the Masses, but was harassed by palace eunuchs and their clients. Under pressure, he resigned from office a few weeks later. In AD 185, Chen Dan was slandered by the eunuchs and sent to prison. He died there. His death was lamentable; as an official, Chen Dan was loyal and honest.

full_text:
Chen Dan (Hangong) 陳耽 (漢公)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Chen Dan (陳耽), styledHangong (漢公), was born in Donghai. Loyal Minister that, due to slander by the eunuchs, was arrested and died in jail. Chen Dan was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Chen Dan lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Chen Dan was a high official under the Han. He served as Minister of Ceremonies, but was often promoted to various other ranks. Chen Dan tried to make a report after being promoted to Minister over the Masses, but was harassed by palace eunuchs and their clients. Under pressure, he resigned from office a few weeks later. In AD 185, Chen Dan was slandered by the eunuchs and sent to prison. He died there. His death was lamentable; as an official, Chen Dan was loyal and honest.
